title: Base-bound queue materialization provenance

memory.observed: The existing manifest-bound materializer verifies exact update bytes and manifest structure, but its output did not independently retain a digest of the exact base queue bytes used.
memory.inferred: A later reviewer could reproduce the update side while unknowingly using a different base snapshot, producing an ambiguous continuity lineage.
memory.proposed: Require a receipt binding the raw base queue, update manifest, and materialized output by SHA-256 before any canonical promotion decision.
memory.superseded: Manifest binding by itself is sufficient provenance for canonical queue promotion.
memory.unresolved: Independent enumeration completeness, cryptographic signer identity, and semantic truth of queue claims remain outside the receipt.

artifact.verification: 7 deterministic Node assertions passed in an isolated runtime using byte-identical source and test content before commit.

falsification_route: Change one byte in the base queue, manifest, or materialized output and verify the corresponding receipt subject digest changes; supply a mismatched manifest digest or authority escalation and verify fail-closed rejection.
